name: Makkah to Madinah Taxi Transfer
description: Private intercity taxi from Makkah to Madinah — 430 km route via Route 60, fixed prices from SAR 500 for sedan. Includes one complimentary prayer stop. Full-day journey with door-to-door hotel service.

offers.name: Sedan Transfer — Makkah to Madinah
offers.description: Toyota Camry or Hyundai Sonata — 1 to 3 passengers, 2 large suitcases. Journey: 430 km, 4.5–5 hours off-peak.

offers.name: SUV/MPV Transfer — Makkah to Madinah
offers.description: Hyundai Staria or GMC Yukon — 4 to 6 passengers, 4 large suitcases.

offers.name: Van Transfer — Makkah to Madinah
offers.description: Toyota Hiace — 7 to 9 passengers, 6 large suitcases. Accommodates Zamzam water containers.

mainEntity.name: How much does a taxi from Makkah to Madinah cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A sedan costs SAR 500–650 in standard season. An SUV or 7-seat MPV costs SAR 700–900. A 9-seat Hiace van costs SAR 900–1,200. Prices increase by 20–30% during Ramadan and Hajj season, per market rates observed in February 2026.

mainEntity.name: How long is the drive from Makkah to Madinah?

acceptedAnswer.text: The journey is 430 km via Route 60 and takes 4.5 to 5 hours off-peak. Expect 6–7 hours when departing Makkah during Ramadan nights or Hajj season due to congestion exiting the Haram area.

mainEntity.name: Is the Makkah to Madinah taxi a shared or private service?

acceptedAnswer.text: The service is fully private. The vehicle travels exclusively for your group with no shared seats and no additional stops for other passengers.

mainEntity.name: Can the driver stop at the Prophet's Mosque on arrival in Madinah?

acceptedAnswer.text: The driver can drop you at the nearest vehicle access point to your Madinah hotel. Direct access to the pedestrian zone around Masjid an-Nabawi is restricted to vehicles.

mainEntity.name: Do I pay the full price for an empty seat?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. The price is per vehicle, not per seat. A family of three in a sedan pays the sedan price; a couple pays the same sedan price. If you have seven passengers, book the Hiace at the van rate.

mainEntity.name: Can we stop at Bir Ali Miqat on the way from Makkah to Madinah?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. The Masjid al-Shajarah at Abyar Ali (Dhul Hulayfah), approximately 9 km southwest of Madinah, is the Miqat for pilgrims. If you need this stop, specify it at booking. The stop adds 20–30 minutes to the journey.
